verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When He had said these words unto them, He abode still in Galilee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when His brethren were gone up, then went He up also unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The things done by Christ after the manner of men, are not so done only to establish the Incarnation, but also to educate us for virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He done all as God, how could we have known, on falling in with such things as we wished not, what we must do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As, for instance, when He was in this very place, and the Jews would have killed Him, He came into the midst of them, and so appeased the tumult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now had He done this continually, how should we, not being able to do so, and yet falling into the like case, have known in what way we ought to deal with the matter, whether to perish at once, or even to use some contrivance in order that the word might go forward?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since, therefore, we who have no power could not have understood what to do on coming into the midst of our foes, on this account we are taught this very thing by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, saith the Evangelist, Jesus, “when He had said these words, abode in Galilee; but when His brethren were gone up, then went He up also unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The expression, “when His brethren were gone up,” is that of one showing that He chose not to go up with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account He abode where He was, and manifested not Himself, although they in a manner urged Him to do so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did He, who ever spake openly, do so now “as it were in secret”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The writer saith not “secretly,” but, “as it were in secret.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For thus, as I have said, He seemed to be instructing us how to manage matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, apart from this, it was not the same to come among them when heated and restive, as to do so afterwards when the feast was ended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then the Jews sought Him, and said, Where is He?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Excellent truly the good deeds at their feasts! they are eager for murder, and wish to seize Him, even during the feast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At least, in another place they speak thus, “Think ye that He will not come to the feast?” ( John xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 56 ); and here they said, “Where is He?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Through their excessive hatred and enmity they would not even call Him by name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Great was their reverence towards the feast, great their caution.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By occasion of the very feast they wished to entrap Him!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And there was much murmuring among the people concerning Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I think they were exasperated by the place where the miracle had been wrought, and were greatly infuriated and afraid, not so much from anger at what had gone before, as from fear lest He should again work something similar.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But all fell out contrary to what they desired, and against their will they rendered Him conspicuous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And some said, He is a good man; others said, Nay, but He deceiveth the people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Methinks the first of these opinions was that of the many, the other that of the rulers and priests.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to slander Him suited their malice and wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He deceiveth,” say they, “the people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Was it by seeming to work, not really working miracles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But experience witnesses the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Howbeit no man spake openly of Him for fear of the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou everywhere the ruling body corrupted, and the ruled sound indeed in judgment, but not having that proper courage which a multitude especially lacketh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now about the middle of the feast Jesus went up and taught.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the delay He made them more attentive; for they who had sought Him on the first days and said, “Where is He?” when they saw Him suddenly present, observe how they drew near, and were like to press upon Him as He was speaking, both those who said that He was a good man, and those who said that He was not such; the former so as to profit by and admire Him, the latter to lay hold on and detain Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: One party then said, “He deceiveth the people,” by reason of the teaching and the doctrines, not understanding His meaning; the other on account of the miracles said, “He is a good man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He therefore thus came among them when He had slackened their anger, so that they might hear His words at leisure, when passion no longer stopped their ears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He taught, the Evangelist hath not told us; that He taught marvelously, this only he saith, and that He won and brought them over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such was the power of His speech.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they who had said, “He deceiveth the people,” altered their opinion, “and marveled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also they said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observest thou how the Evangelist showeth here also their marveling to be full of wickedness? for he saith not, that they admired the teaching, or that they received the words, but simply that they “marveled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, were thrown into a state of astonishment, and doubted, saying, “Whence hath this man these things”? when they ought from this very difficulty to have known that there was nothing merely human in Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But because they would not confess this, but stopped at wondering only, hear what He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My doctrine is not Mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He answereth to their secret thoughts, referring them to the Father, and so desiring to stop their mouths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any man will do His will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of Myself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is this, “Cast out from yourselves the malice and wrath and envy and hatred which has without cause been conceived against Me, then there is nothing to hinder you from knowing that My words are indeed the words of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For at present these things cast a darkness over you, and destroy the light of right judgment, while if ye remove them this shall no longer be your case.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He spake not (plainly) thus, (for so He would have confounded them exceedingly,) but implied it all by saying, “He that doeth His will shall know of the doctrine, whether it is of God, or whether I speak of Myself”; that is, “whether I speak anything different and strange and contrary to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “of Myself” is always put with this meaning, that “I say nothing except what seemeth good to Him, but all that the Father willeth, I will also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any man do His will, he shall know of the doctrine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What meaneth,” “If any man do His will?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any man be a lover of the life which is according to virtue, he shall know the power of the sayings.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any man will give heed to the prophecies, to see whether I speak according to them or not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how is the doctrine His and not His?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He said not, “This doctrine is not Mine”; but having first said, “it is Mine,” and having claimed it as His own, He then added, “it is not Mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then can the same thing be both “His” and not “His”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is “His,” because He spake it not as one who had been taught; and it is “not His,” because it was the doctrine of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith He, “All that is the Father’s is Mine, and Mine His”? ( c. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if because the doctrine is the Father’s, it is not thine, that other assertion is false, for according to that it ought to be thine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the “is not Mine,” affords a strong proof that His doctrine and the Father’s are one; as if He had said, “It hath nothing different, as though it were another’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For though My Person be different, yet so do I speak and do as not to be supposed to speak or do anything contrary to the Father, but rather the very same things that the Father saith and doeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He addeth another incontrovertible argument, bringing forward something merely human, and instructing them by things to which they were accustomed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “He that desireth to establish any doctrine of his own, desireth to do so only that he himself may enjoy the glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if I desire not to enjoy glory, wherefore should I desire to establish any doctrine of My own?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that speaketh of himself, that is, who speaketh anything peculiar or different from others, speaketh on this account, that he may establish his own glory; but if I seek the glory of Him that sent Me, wherefore should I choose to teach other things?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that there was a cause wherefore He said there too that He “did nothing of Himself”? ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19, and viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What was it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was that they might believe that He desired not the honor of the many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when His words are lowly, “I seek,” He saith, “the glory of the Father,” everywhere desiring to persuade them that He Himself loveth not glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now there are many reasons for His using lowly words, as that He might not be deemed unbegotten, or opposed to God, His being clothed with flesh, the infirmity of His hearers, that He might teach men to be modest, and to speak no great thing of themselves: while for speaking lofty words one could only find one reason, the greatness of His Nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if when He said, “Before Abraham was, I am” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 58 ), they were offended, what would have been their case if they had continually heard high expressions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Did not Moses give you the Law? and yet none of you keepeth the Law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why go ye about to kill Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And what connection,” saith some one, “has this, or what has this to do with what was said before?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews brought against Him two accusations; one, that He broke the Sabbath; the other, that He called God His Father, making Himself equal with God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that this was no imagination of theirs, but His own declared judgment, and that He spake not as do the many, but in a special and peculiar sense, is clear from this circumstance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many often called God their Father; as “Have we not all one Father, hath not one God created us?” ( Mal. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ), but not for that was the people equal to God, on which account the hearers were not offended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then when the Jews said, “This man is not from God,” He often healed them, and made defense for the violation of the Sabbath; so now had the sense they assigned to His words been according to their imagination, not according to His intention, He would have corrected them, and said, “Why suppose ye Me equal to God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I am not equal”; yet He said nothing of the kind, but, on the contrary, declared by what followed, that He is equal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “As the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 21 ); and “That all may honor the Son as they honor the Father”; and “The works which He doeth, the same doeth the Son likewise;” all these go to establish His equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, concerning the Law He saith, “Think not that I am come to destroy the Law or the Prophets.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus He knoweth how to remove evil suspicions which are in their minds; but in this place He not only doth not remove, but even confirmeth their suspicion of His equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account also, when they said in another place, “Thou makest thyself God,” He did not remove their suspicion, but even confirmed it, saying, “That ye may know that the Son of Man hath power on earth to forgive sins, He saith to the sick of the palsy, Take up thy bed, and walk.” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This then He first aimed at, to make Himself equal with God, showing that He was not God’s adversary, but that He said the same and taught the same with Him, and afterwards He setteth Himself to the breach of the Sabbath, saying, “Did not Moses give you the Law, and none of you keepeth the Law?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “The Law saith, Thou shalt not kill; but ye kill, and yet accuse Me as transgressing the Law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore saith He, “None of you”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they all sought to kill Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And if,” He saith, “I even have broken the Law, it was in saving a man, but ye transgress it for evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if My action was even a transgression, yet it was in order to save, and I ought not to be judged by you who transgress in the greatest matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For your conduct is a subverting of the whole Law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then also He presseth it farther, although He had said many things to them before, but at that former time He spake after a loftier manner, and more suitably to His own dignity, while now He speaketh more humbly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He would not continually irritate them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At present their anger had become intense, and they went on to murder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore He continueth to check them in these two ways, by reproving their evil daring, and saying, “Why go ye about to kill Me?” and by modestly calling Himself, “A Man that hath told you the truth” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 40 ), and by showing that murderers in heart are not worthy to judge others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe both the humility of Christ’s question, and the insolence of their answer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou hast a devil; who goeth about to kill thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The expression is one of wrath and anger, and of a soul made shameless by an unexpected reproof, and put to confusion before their time, as they thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For just as a sort of robbers who sing over their plots, then when they desire to put him against whom they are plotting off his guard, effect their object by keeping silence, so also do these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He, omitting to rebuke them for this, so as not to make them more shameless, again taketh in hand His defense with respect to the Sabbath, reasoning with them from the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how prudently.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “No wonder,” He saith, “if ye disobey Me, when ye disobey the Law which ye think ye obey, and which ye hold to have been given you by Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is therefore no new thing, if ye give not heed to My words.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because they said, “God spake to Moses, but as for this fellow we know not whence he is” ( c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ), He showeth that they were insulting Moses as well as Himself, for Moses gave them the Law, and they obeyed it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have done one work, and ye all marvel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how He argueth, where it is necessary to defend Himself, and make His defense a charge against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For with respect to that which had been wrought, He introduceth not the Person of the Father, but His own: “I have done one work.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He would show, that not to have done it would have been to break the Law, and that there are many things more authoritative than the Law, and that “Moses” endured to receive a command against the Law, and more authoritative than the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “circumcision” is more authoritative than the Sabbath, and yet circumcision is not of the Law, but of “the fathers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But I,” He saith, “have done that which is more authoritative and better than circumcision.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He mentioneth not the command of the Law; for instance, that the Priests profane the Sabbath, as He had said already, but speaketh more largely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The meaning of, “Ye marvel” ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ) is, “Ye are confused,” “are troubled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the Law was to be lasting, circumcision would not have been more authoritative than it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He said not, “I have done a thing greater than circumcision,” but abundantly refuteth them by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If a man receive circumcision.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Seest thou that the Law is most established when a man breaketh it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the breaking of the Sabbath is the keeping of the Law? that if the Sabbath were not broken, the Law must needs have been broken? so that I also have established the Law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Ye are wroth with Me because I have wrought a thing which is greater than circumcision,” but having merely mentioned what had been done, He left it to them to judge, whether entire health was not a more necessary thing than circumcision.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Law,” He saith, “is broken, that a man may receive a sign which contributeth nothing to health; are ye vexed and indignant at its being broken, that one might be freed from so grievous a disease?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Judge not according to appearance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is, “according to appearance”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Do not, since Moses hath the greatest honor among you, give your decision according to your estimation of persons, but according to the nature of things; for this is to judge rightly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore hath no one of you reproved Moses?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore hath no one disobeyed him when he ordereth that the Sabbath be broken by a commandment introduced from without into the Law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He alloweth a commandment to be of more authority than his own Law; a commandment not introduced by the Law, but from without, which is especially wonderful; while ye who are not lawgivers are beyond measure jealous for the Law, and defend it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet Moses, who ordereth that the Law be broken by a commandment which is not of the Law, is more worthy of confidence than you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By saying then, (I have made) “a whole man (healthy),” He showeth that circumcision also was “partial” health.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what was the health procured by circumcision?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Every soul,” It saith, “that is not circumcised, shall be utterly destroyed.” ( Gen. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But I have raised up a man not partially afflicted, but wholly undone.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Judge not,” therefore, “according to appearance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Be we persuaded that this is said not merely to the men of that time, but to us also, that in nothing we pervert justice, but do all in its behalf; that whether a man be poor or rich, we give no heed to persons, but enquire into things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not pity,” It saith, “the poor in judgment.” ( Ex. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is meant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Be not broken down, nor bent,” It saith, “if he that doth the wrong be a poor man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if you may not favor a poor man, much less a rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this I say not only to you who are judges, but to all men, that they nowhere pervert justice, but preserve it everywhere pure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Lord,” It saith, “loveth righteousness”; and, “he that loveth iniquity hateth his own soul.” ( Ps. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 and 5 , LXX.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not, I entreat, hate our own souls, nor love unrighteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For certainly its profit in the present world is little or nothing, and for the world to come it brings great damage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, I should say, that not even here can we enjoy it; for when we live softly, yet with an evil conscience, is not this vengeance and punishment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then love righteousness, and never look aside from that law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what fruit shall we gain from the present life, if we depart without having attained unto excellence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What there will help us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will friendship, or relations, or this or that man’s favor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What am I saying? this or that man’s favor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Though we have Noah, Job, or Daniel for a father, this will avail us nothing if we be betrayed by our own works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: One thing alone we need, that is, excellency of soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This will be able to carry you safe through, and to deliver you from everlasting fire, this will escort you to the Kingdom of Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To which may we all attain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever and world without end.
